name: patch
context: Patch exact dependency versions with pnpm and commit repeatable fixes into the repository
info: This skill covers pnpm's patch workflow: extracting an exact package version into an editable directory, committing a generated patch file, and removing obsolete patches later. It matters when upstream is broken, a transitive dependency needs a targeted fix, or a build must be unblocked without forking a package. Patch is different from overrides: it changes package contents, not just resolved versions.
triggers: patch a broken dependency in pnpm
fix a transitive package without forking
body:
# pnpm Patch Workflow

The local pnpm 10.23.0 CLI help shows the three moving parts clearly:

```bash
pnpm patch <pkg>@<version>
pnpm patch-commit <patchDir>
pnpm patch-remove <pkg...>
```

Patch when the package contents themselves need to change. If the problem is only version selection, prefer catalogs or overrides from [[pnpm/config#catalogs-and-overrides]].

sections.title: Start a patch
sections.body: Use an exact package spec such as `esbuild@0.25.1`. The local help for `pnpm patch` supports `--edit-dir` to control where the extracted package is written and `--ignore-existing` when an earlier patch already exists. After extraction, edit the files in that directory rather than touching `node_modules` directly.

sections.title: Commit and remove patches
sections.body: `pnpm patch-commit <patchDir>` generates the patch file and updates manifest state such as `patchedDependencies`. `pnpm patch-remove <pkg...>` removes the patch definition when upstream no longer needs local edits. Commit the patch file and manifest changes together so future installs reproduce the fix exactly.

sections.title: Patch vs override
sections.body: Use a patch when code or packaged assets must change. Use overrides or catalogs when only the chosen version should change. Use [[pnpm/security]] when the issue is whether install-time scripts should be allowed to run at all. These are different control surfaces and should not be mixed casually.

links.context: Build-script trust and patching solve different classes of dependency problems
Patch workflows happen after the dependency graph is materialized
Use overrides or catalogs when version selection is the real problem

related.note: Patching starts from an installed or resolvable exact dependency version
Patching a dependency is different from approving or blocking its install-time scripts

scripts.name: Manage Patch
scripts.description: Start, commit, or remove pnpm dependency patches with exact package specs

PNPM_HOME.description: Optional pnpm home directory if the binary is not on the default PATH

packages.description: Exact package specs, mainly for start or remove

patchDir.description: Edited directory passed to pnpm patch-commit

patchesDir.description: Optional output directory for generated patch files

depends.reason: Patch workflows assume a real dependency graph and exact package versions

author: dojo-community
